E.description: Single-shot compressive video forward model: a temporally varying binary coded mask gates photons per-pixel for each sub-frame, and the detector integrates all B masked sub-frames into a single 2D snapshot exposure.
E.world_state_x: 3D video cube x in R^{H x W x B}, non-negative, compressible in spatio-temporal basis (e.g., 3D wavelet, sparse optical flow)
E.observation_y: 2D sensor snapshot y in R^{H x W} at effective frame rate 1/B of the sub-frame rate (Poisson+Gaussian noise)
physical_parameters_theta: C_b: binary coded mask per sub-frame b in {0,1}^{H x W}
detector exposure time T, sensor gain gamma, offset o
mask duty cycle eta, clock offset dt
sub-pixel registration (dx, dy) and rotation theta

G._L_DAG_derivation: (|V|-1) + log10(kappa_sys/kappa_0) + n_c = 1 + log10(2500/1000) + 0 = 1 + 0.398 = 1.398 (rounded to 1.4). kappa_0=1000 is the registry-wide convention (established by CASSI #25); CACTI satisfies kappa_sys >= kappa_0 so no max(0, .) clamp applies.

W.regime: underdetermined B:1 compressive video recovery; binary random temporally-varying masks satisfy RIP-like conditions; sub-pixel mask mismatch is dominant stability risk (10.4x residual ratio observed, EfficientSCI drops 20.58 dB under severe mismatch).

C.solver_class: iterative_proximal (GAP-TV, DeSCI) or plug-and-play (PnP-FFDNet) or learned unrolling (EfficientSCI, BIRNAT)
C.error_bound: ||x_hat - x||_2 <= C1 * sigma_noise / sqrt(m) + C2 * ||x - x_s||_1; per-frame PSNR recovery ratio = 1.00 with GAP-TV at 60s calibration.
C.complexity: O(H * W * B * log(H*W*B)) per iteration for FFT-based proximal steps; O(H*W*B) for denoising priors.
